title: Terminal int8 sigmoid block
hypothesis: A bounded int8 sigmoid activation block can be implemented and physically characterized well enough to serve as the Layer 1 prerequisite for later fixed-nm1 terminal nonlinear activation evaluation.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one bounded int8 sigmoid block produce accepted physical implementation metrics suitable for later Layer 2 evaluation use?
include: one bounded int8 sigmoid block candidate
one bounded physical sweep on the target platform
exclude: multiple nonlinear blocks in the first pass
fp16-native sigmoid in the first pass
follow_on_broad_sweep: only if the first sigmoid block is accepted and further circuit-family exploration becomes necessary

expected_benefit: provides real physical timing, power, and area for a bounded int8 nonlinear activation block
unblocks a technically credible Layer 2 terminal activation-family proposal
risks: the bounded int8 sigmoid may still be too costly or too inaccurate in the first chosen implementation style
the first pass may need wrapper or interface adjustments before it is consumable by Layer 2
later fp16-end-to-end use may still require an explicit conversion strategy or a separate native-fp16 follow-on block

expected_result.reason: the first goal is simply to obtain accepted physical implementation metrics for one bounded int8 sigmoid block
